name: Stuffed Cabbage Italian Style (Microwave)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the ribs from each cabbage leaf very thin. In an 11-inch by 7-inch baking dish, cook the cabbage leaves and 2 tablespoons water on High for 8 to 10 minutes until tender; drain. Set aside.
2. In a 2-quart casserole, cook oil and onion on High for 3 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook covered for an additional 3 to 4 minutes until tender. Stir in uncooked rice, cheese, salt, and pepper.
3. On the center of each cabbage leaf, place about 1/3 cup rice mixture. Fold the bottom of the leaf over the filling, then fold the sides toward the center. Roll tightly, jelly-roll fashion.
4. In an 11-inch by 7-inch baking dish, arrange the cabbage rolls seam-side down. Pour spaghetti sauce over the cabbage rolls. Cook covered on High for 8 to 12 minutes until tender, rotating the dish halfway through cooking. Sprinkle rolls with mozzarella cheese and let stand, covered, for 3 minutes. Makes 8 cabbage rolls.
Each roll: About 225 calories, 9 grams fat, 8 milligrams cholesterol, 495 milligrams sodium.
Note: I find that this was relatively bland, so I tend to add lots more pepper and a bit more Parmesan cheese to the mixture.
